title: NEWBORN RESUSCITATION (NRP) and TEAM LEADERSHIP in the Delivery Room for a Term Newborn Boy Born by EMERGENT CESAREAN for a Category III Fetal Heart Tracing (Recurrent Late Decelerations + Minimal Variability) Who Arrives APNEIC, FLOPPY, BLUE, and BRADYCARDIC (HR ~70) — Pre-Resuscitation HUDDLE and Equipment CHECK Before Delivery, CLOSED-LOOP ROLE ASSIGNMENT (Team Leader + Airway/RT + Compressor + Vascular-Access/Medications + Documenter + Family Liaison), the GOLDEN MINUTE Sequence of INITIAL STEPS (Warm + Dry + Position + Clear Airway PRN + Stimulate), Beginning POSITIVE-PRESSURE VENTILATION at 40-60/min with ROOM AIR for a Term Infant When HR < 100 or Apneic/Gasping, CORRECTING Inadequate PPV with MR SOPA (Mask + Reposition + Suction + Open Mouth + Pressure + Alternative Airway), Pulse Oximetry on the RIGHT WRIST (Preductal), Adding CHEST COMPRESSIONS at a 3:1 Ratio (Two-Thumb Encircling) When HR < 60 After 30 Seconds of Effective PPV, EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO via Rapidly Placed Umbilical Vein Catheter When HR < 60 Despite Compressions, Therapeutic-Hypothermia Eligibility Screening, NICU Hand-Off Using SBAR, Family Communication, and a Team DEBRIEF
subtitle: Newborn resuscitation is a high-stakes, low-frequency event for which the QUALITY of TEAM PREPARATION and LEADERSHIP is the single biggest predictor of outcome. Approximately 10% of newborns need some help to begin breathing at birth and about 1% need extensive resuscitation; the FIRST MINUTE — the 'GOLDEN MINUTE' — sets the trajectory. The NEONATAL RESUSCITATION PROGRAM (NRP) provides a standardized algorithm that the team rehearses, anticipates, and executes together. PRE-RESUSCITATION preparation (before the baby is delivered) is itself a leadership act: the team performs a HUDDLE that identifies the at-risk delivery (here, an emergent cesarean for a Category III FHR tracing), assigns CLOSED-LOOP ROLES (team leader, airway/RT, chest-compression provider, vascular-access/medications, documenter/recorder, family liaison), verifies a PRE-WARMED radiant warmer + warm blankets + plastic wrap if preterm < 32 weeks + suction with bulb/catheter + appropriately sized bag-mask + appropriately sized endotracheal tubes (2.5/3.0/3.5) + functioning laryngoscope with blades + pulse oximeter + ECG + UVC tray + epinephrine + normal saline, and CONFIRMS gestational age, anticipated weight, and any maternal/fetal risk factors. AT BIRTH the team performs the rapid INITIAL ASSESSMENT — TERM gestation? GOOD TONE? BREATHING or CRYING? — all YES → routine care with mother (warm + dry + skin-to-skin + delayed cord clamping when appropriate). Any NO → bring to the radiant warmer for INITIAL STEPS (about 30 seconds): WARM, DRY (vigorous drying serves as the first stimulation and removes wet linens; for preterm < 32 weeks place into a plastic bag/wrap WITHOUT drying to maintain heat), POSITION the airway in the 'sniffing' position, CLEAR the airway only if obstructed (mouth before nose; bulb syringe or suction catheter), and STIMULATE (rubbing the back, flicking the soles); avoid hyperthermia. EVALUATE breathing and HEART RATE (auscultate at the precordium for 6 seconds and multiply by 10, or use ECG leads). HR ≥ 100 with effective breathing → continue routine. HR < 100 OR apneic/gasping → begin POSITIVE-PRESSURE VENTILATION (PPV) within the golden minute at a rate of 40-60 breaths/minute (chanted as 'Breathe . . . Two . . . Three . . .') with ROOM AIR (FiO2 21%) for term infants and FiO2 21-30% for preterm; place a pulse oximeter on the RIGHT WRIST (PREDUCTAL) and an ECG for accurate HR. The single most important sign of effective PPV is a RISING HEART RATE; CHEST RISE is reassuring but HR is the metric. If HR is not rising, perform the corrective steps in MR SOPA order: (M) Mask adjust to a good seal, (R) Reposition the airway, (S) Suction mouth/nose, (O) Open the mouth, (P) Pressure increase, (A) Alternative airway (intubate or place a laryngeal mask). If HR remains < 60 after 30 SECONDS of EFFECTIVE PPV, start CHEST COMPRESSIONS — preferred two-thumb-encircling-hands technique on the lower third of the sternum at a 3:1 ratio (3 compressions : 1 breath) for a total of 120 events per minute (90 compressions + 30 breaths), depth about one third of the AP chest diameter; intubate or place an alternative airway if not already done, and switch FiO2 to 100% once compressions begin. Reassess HR after 60 seconds of compressions + PPV. If HR remains < 60, give EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (0.2 mL/kg of the 0.1 mg/mL solution) — via an UMBILICAL VEIN CATHETER (UVC) for fastest access, or IO if UVC fails; the endotracheal route at 0.1 mg/kg may be used briefly while IV/IO is being established but is less reliable; repeat every 3-5 minutes as needed. Consider a volume bolus of normal saline 10 mL/kg IV/IO if hypovolemia is suspected (blood loss/abruption/cord accident). Continue resuscitation; if there is no detectable heart rate after 20 minutes of complete and effective resuscitation, withdrawing is reasonable in consultation with the team and family. THERMOREGULATION is preserved throughout — radiant warmer, pre-warmed blankets, plastic wrap for preterm < 32 weeks, avoid hyperthermia (which worsens HIE). Once stabilized, screen for therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility (≥ 36 weeks gestation, within 6 hours of birth, with evidence of hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy) and arrange NICU admission. Communicate with the family as soon as possible — honest, brief updates from the leader or family-liaison nurse; bring the family to the bedside as feasible. After the event, a STRUCTURED DEBRIEF identifies what went well, what to do differently, and supports the team and the family. KEY LEADERSHIP behaviors: BRIEF the team before delivery; assign CLOSED-LOOP ROLES with named directed tasks and required readbacks; protect the AIRWAY first; the QUALITY of PPV is the most common difference between success and failure (MR SOPA); start COMPRESSIONS only when HR < 60 despite 30 seconds of EFFECTIVE PPV; use the UVC for fastest medication access; communicate using SBAR for the NICU hand-off; and protect thermoregulation throughout. PERTINENT MISTAKES TO AVOID: starting compressions before the airway/PPV is effective (the cause of bradycardia in newborns is almost always hypoxia from inadequate ventilation); using a 30:2 ratio (that is for older infants/children/adults — newborn is 3:1); starting at 100% O2 in a term newborn (start at 21%, escalate as needed); skipping the right-wrist pulse oximeter (preductal); forgetting MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective; suctioning vigorously or deeply before stimulation; routinely intubating and suctioning meconium-stained non-vigorous newborns (no longer routine — only if needed for ventilation); allowing hyperthermia; over-explaining clinical details to the family at the bedside; and skipping the team debrief. NURSING ROLE in the bedside team: as team leader (when an RN is the designated leader for the initial response) — pre-deliver brief; assign closed-loop roles; lead the algorithm; protect thermoregulation; place pulse oximeter on the right wrist; ensure effective PPV with MR SOPA; coordinate compressions and meds with UVC access; communicate honestly with the family; hand off to the NICU using SBAR; lead the debrief. As a team member — execute the assigned role with closed-loop communication; speak up about safety concerns; advocate for the newborn and family.

patient.name: Baby Boy A. Sundaram
patient.code: Full code at this time (parents in agreement); goal = coordinated newborn resuscitation per NRP — pre-delivery huddle + equipment check + closed-loop role assignment; rapid initial assessment + initial steps within ~30 sec; PPV within the Golden Minute for HR < 100 or apnea (room air for term; right-wrist pulse ox + ECG; MR SOPA to correct ineffective PPV); chest compressions 3:1 if HR < 60 after 30 sec of effective PPV; UVC + epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO if HR < 60 despite compressions; thermoregulation + therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility screening; SBAR hand-off to NICU; family communication; team debrief
patient.admitDate: Term newborn boy delivered by EMERGENT cesarean for a Category III FHR tracing (recurrent LATE decelerations with minimal variability) in a 28-year-old G1P0 mother at 39+2 weeks with intrapartum maternal fever 38.7°C suggestive of chorioamnionitis. The NRP team was pre-briefed in the delivery room: closed-loop roles assigned (team leader, airway/RT, chest-compression provider, vascular-access/medications, documenter, family liaison); pre-warmed radiant warmer + warm blankets + suction + size-3 BMV + appropriate ETT sizes (2.5/3.0/3.5) + laryngoscope + pulse oximeter + ECG + UVC tray + epinephrine + normal saline VERIFIED. At birth the newborn is term, FLOPPY (poor tone), APNEIC (no spontaneous respirations), CYANOTIC, with HR ~70 by precordial auscultation — the NRP algorithm is initiated.
patient.room: Delivery room/OR; pre-warmed radiant warmer + resuscitation table; NRP team in place; NICU on standby; family at bedside per OR policy; bereavement/social work available as needed

vitals.temp: Pre-warmed radiant warmer; team huddle complete; closed-loop roles assigned (leader/airway/compressor/access-meds/documenter/family liaison); equipment verified; gestational age + risk factors confirmed; family informed

vitals.bp: Not routinely measured at this stage
vitals.spo2: Pulse oximeter being placed on the RIGHT WRIST (preductal)
vitals.temp: Term gestation + POOR TONE + APNEIC → bring to radiant warmer for INITIAL STEPS

vitals.rr: Apneic — no spontaneous respirations after drying/stimulation
vitals.spo2: Pulse oximeter on right wrist; ECG leads on (for accurate HR)
vitals.temp: WARMED + DRIED (vigorous drying serves as first stimulation; wet linens removed); POSITIONED in sniffing position; airway suctioned ONLY because obstructed (mouth before nose, bulb); STIMULATED (back rub, flick soles) — no response → begin PPV

vitals.time: PPV at 40-60/min with room air (term) + MR SOPA when HR not rising
vitals.temp: PPV begun within the GOLDEN MINUTE at 40-60/min with ROOM AIR (FiO2 21%) for a term infant; chest rise initially poor → MR SOPA executed in order: M (mask seal), R (reposition airway), S (suction mouth/nose), O (open mouth), P (pressure ↑), A (alternative airway = intubation/LMA) — alternative airway placed after PPV remains ineffective; HR begins to rise

vitals.time: Chest compressions 3:1 (HR remains < 60 after 30 sec effective PPV)
vitals.temp: Compressions begun (two-thumb-encircling-hands technique, lower third of sternum, ~1/3 AP chest depth) at 3:1 ratio (3 compressions : 1 breath) for 120 events/min; FiO2 increased to 100%; reassess HR after 60 sec of effective compressions + PPV

vitals.time: UVC + epinephrine (HR < 60 despite compressions) → ROSC + NICU hand-off + debrief
vitals.temp: Umbilical vein catheter placed (fastest newborn access); EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV (0.2 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL) given; consider NS 10 mL/kg IV bolus if hypovolemia suspected; HR rises to 120 with ROSC; thermoregulation preserved; therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility screened (≥ 36 wk + < 6 h of birth + evidence of HIE); NICU hand-off via SBAR; family updated; team DEBRIEF held

labs.n: PRE-RESUSCITATION huddle + equipment check + closed-loop role assignment
labs.v: Pre-resuscitation preparation is itself a leadership act. The team HUDDLES BEFORE delivery to identify the at-risk situation (here, an emergent cesarean for a Category III FHR tracing with intrapartum maternal fever); ASSIGN CLOSED-LOOP ROLES (TEAM LEADER, AIRWAY/RT, CHEST-COMPRESSION provider, VASCULAR-ACCESS/MEDICATIONS, DOCUMENTER/RECORDER, FAMILY LIAISON); VERIFY equipment (pre-warmed radiant warmer + warm blankets + plastic wrap if preterm < 32 weeks + suction with bulb/catheter + appropriately sized BMV + ETT sizes 2.5/3.0/3.5 + functioning laryngoscope with blades + pulse oximeter + ECG + UVC tray + epinephrine + normal saline); and CONFIRM gestational age, anticipated weight, and maternal/fetal risk factors. CLOSED-LOOP communication uses named recipients, directed tasks, and repeat-back/confirmation. The team leader's job is to keep the OVERVIEW, prevent freelancing, protect the airway-first sequence, time the algorithm, and communicate with the family — not to perform every task.
labs.r: PRE-DELIVERY: huddle + identify at-risk delivery + assign CLOSED-LOOP roles (leader + airway/RT + compressor + access/meds + documenter + family liaison) + verify equipment (warmer/blankets/plastic wrap if preterm<32/suction/BMV/ETT 2.5-3.5/laryngoscope/pulse ox/ECG/UVC tray/epi/NS) + confirm GA/weight/risks. Closed-loop = named + directed + repeat-back + confirm. Team leader keeps overview, doesn't freelance. RN role: brief + delegate + lead.

labs.n: INITIAL ASSESSMENT + initial steps + PPV within the GOLDEN MINUTE + MR SOPA + right-wrist pulse ox
labs.v: AT BIRTH the rapid initial assessment is three questions: TERM gestation? GOOD TONE? BREATHING or CRYING? Any NO → bring to the radiant warmer. INITIAL STEPS (~30 seconds): WARM (radiant warmer; plastic wrap for preterm < 32 weeks without drying first); DRY vigorously (the first stimulation; removes wet linens); POSITION the airway in a sniffing position; CLEAR the airway only if obstructed (mouth before nose; bulb or suction catheter); STIMULATE (rub the back, flick the soles); avoid hyperthermia. EVALUATE breathing and HEART RATE (auscultate 6 seconds × 10 or use ECG leads). HR ≥ 100 + effective breathing → routine care; HR < 100 OR apnea/gasping → begin PPV within the GOLDEN MINUTE at 40-60 breaths/min ('Breathe . . . Two . . . Three . . .') with ROOM AIR (FiO2 21%) for term infants (FiO2 21-30% preterm). Place the PULSE OXIMETER on the RIGHT WRIST (PREDUCTAL) and use ECG for accurate HR. The single most important sign of effective PPV is a RISING HEART RATE; chest rise is reassuring but HR is the metric. If HR is not rising, perform CORRECTIVE STEPS in MR SOPA order: M (mask seal), R (reposition airway), S (suction mouth/nose), O (open mouth), P (pressure increase), A (alternative airway — intubate or place a laryngeal mask). Effective PPV alone reverses most newborn bradycardia.
labs.r: Birth questions: TERM? TONE? BREATHING/crying? Any NO → warmer for initial steps (WARM + DRY (first stim, remove wet linens; preterm <32 wk plastic wrap w/o drying) + POSITION sniffing + CLEAR airway only if obstructed (mouth before nose) + STIMULATE). Evaluate respiration + HR (6 sec auscultate ×10 or ECG). HR ≥100 + breathing → routine. HR <100 OR apnea/gasping → PPV within Golden Minute at 40-60/min with ROOM AIR (21%) for term (21-30% preterm). Pulse ox on RIGHT WRIST (preductal) + ECG. Best sign of effective PPV = RISING HR (not just chest rise). If HR not rising → MR SOPA: Mask seal / Reposition / Suction / Open mouth / Pressure ↑ / Alternative airway. RN role: PPV is the most important intervention.

labs.n: Chest compressions (3:1) + UVC + EPINEPHRINE + volume + thermoregulation + SBAR hand-off + debrief
labs.v: If the HR remains < 60 after 30 SECONDS of EFFECTIVE PPV, start CHEST COMPRESSIONS — preferred two-thumb-encircling-hands technique on the LOWER THIRD of the sternum at a 3:1 ratio (3 COMPRESSIONS : 1 BREATH) for a total of 120 events per minute (90 compressions + 30 breaths), depth about ONE THIRD of the AP chest diameter; intubate or place an alternative airway if not already done; switch to FiO2 100% once compressions begin. Reassess HR after 60 SECONDS of effective compressions + PPV. If HR remains < 60, give EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (0.2 mL/kg of the 0.1 mg/mL solution) via UMBILICAL VEIN CATHETER (UVC, the fastest access in the newborn), or via IO if UVC fails. The endotracheal route at 0.1 mg/kg may be used briefly while IV/IO is being established but is less reliable. REPEAT epinephrine every 3-5 minutes as needed. Consider a VOLUME bolus of NORMAL SALINE 10 mL/kg IV/IO if hypovolemia is suspected (blood loss/abruption/cord accident); blood may be used if available. If there is no detectable heart rate after 20 minutes of complete and effective resuscitation, withdrawing is reasonable in consultation with the team and family. THERMOREGULATION is preserved throughout (radiant warmer; pre-warmed blankets; plastic wrap for preterm < 32 weeks; avoid hyperthermia, which worsens HIE). Once stabilized, screen for THERAPEUTIC HYPOTHERMIA eligibility (≥ 36 weeks gestation, within 6 HOURS of birth, with evidence of hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy on standardized exam ± blood gas). Hand off to the NICU using SBAR (Situation/Background/Assessment/Recommendation). COMMUNICATE with the family as soon as possible — honest, brief updates from the leader or family-liaison nurse; bring the family to the bedside as feasible. After the event, lead a STRUCTURED DEBRIEF.
labs.r: Compressions if HR <60 after 30 sec of EFFECTIVE PPV: TWO-THUMB ENCIRCLING preferred, lower third sternum, ~1/3 AP depth, 3:1 ratio = 90 comp + 30 breaths/min = 120 events/min; intubate or place alt airway; FiO2 100% with compressions. Reassess HR after 60 sec compressions + PPV. If HR <60 → EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (0.2 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL) via UVC (fastest newborn access) or IO; ET 0.1 mg/kg less reliable bridge. Repeat q3-5 min. VOLUME NS 10 mL/kg if hypovolemia. After 20 min of complete effective resuscitation w/ no detectable HR, withdrawing is reasonable. Thermoregulation preserved (no hyperthermia). Therapeutic hypothermia screen if ≥36 wk + <6 h + HIE. SBAR NICU handoff. Family communication. Team DEBRIEF. RN role: lead the protocol + handoff + family + debrief.

labs.n: Pertinent NEGATIVES + protective factors + family communication + bereavement readiness
labs.v: PERTINENT NEGATIVES (correct emergency practice): the team did NOT start chest compressions before PPV was effective (the cause of newborn bradycardia is almost always hypoxia from inadequate ventilation); did NOT use the older 30:2 ratio (that is for older infants/children/adults — newborn is 3:1); did NOT start at 100% O2 in a term newborn (start at 21%, escalate as needed); did NOT skip the right-wrist preductal pulse oximeter; did NOT forget MR SOPA when PPV was ineffective; did NOT routinely intubate and tracheally suction a non-vigorous meconium-stained newborn (no longer routine — only if needed for effective ventilation); did NOT allow hyperthermia; did NOT over-explain clinical details to the family at the bedside; and did NOT skip the team debrief or family follow-up. PROTECTIVE FACTORS: pre-resuscitation huddle + equipment verification + closed-loop role assignment; rapid initial assessment + initial steps; airway-first sequence with PPV within the golden minute at correct rate and FiO2 with right-wrist pulse ox + ECG; MR SOPA when ineffective; chest compressions 3:1 only when HR < 60 after effective PPV; UVC + epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg as needed; volume if hypovolemia suspected; thermoregulation preserved; therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility screening when applicable; SBAR NICU hand-off; honest family communication with bereavement/chaplaincy readiness; team debrief. EXPECTED outcome (as here): with effective PPV, brief compressions, and one dose of epinephrine via UVC, the newborn achieves ROSC with spontaneous respirations, transfers safely to NICU, and the family is supported throughout.
labs.r: Negatives: did NOT start compressions before effective PPV, did NOT use 30:2 ratio (newborn = 3:1), did NOT start 100% O2 in term (start 21%), did NOT skip right-wrist preductal pulse ox, did NOT forget MR SOPA, did NOT routinely intubate-suction non-vigorous meconium newborn, did NOT allow hyperthermia, did NOT over-explain at bedside, did NOT skip debrief/family follow-up. Protective: huddle + equipment + closed-loop roles + initial steps + PPV within Golden Minute at correct rate/FiO2 + right-wrist pulse ox + ECG + MR SOPA + compressions 3:1 only when HR <60 after effective PPV + UVC + epi + volume if hypovolemic + thermoregulation + hypothermia eligibility screen + SBAR NICU handoff + honest family + bereavement readiness + team debrief.

labs.v: ~70 (bradycardic — below the < 100 threshold that triggers PPV; below the < 60 threshold for compressions if not corrected)
labs.r: ≥ 100 bpm at birth indicates adequate cardiac response

labs.v: 3 (depressed; record but do NOT delay resuscitation to compute Apgar)
Sent (informs HIE screening and therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility; do not delay resuscitation to obtain)

nursesNote: Delivery room — TERM newborn boy delivered by EMERGENT CESAREAN for Category III FHR tracing (recurrent late decels + minimal variability) in a 28-yo G1P0 at 39+2 wk with maternal fever 38.7°C (likely chorioamnionitis). PRE-DELIVERY HUDDLE complete: closed-loop roles assigned (TEAM LEADER + AIRWAY/RT + CHEST-COMPRESSION provider + VASCULAR-ACCESS/MEDS + DOCUMENTER + FAMILY LIAISON); equipment verified (pre-warmed radiant warmer + warm blankets + suction + BMV + ETT 2.5/3.0/3.5 + laryngoscope + pulse ox + ECG + UVC tray + EPI + NS); GA + weight + maternal/fetal risk factors confirmed. AT BIRTH: term + POOR TONE + APNEIC + cyanotic + HR ~70 — brought to radiant warmer. INITIAL STEPS (~30 sec): WARMED + DRIED vigorously (first stimulation; wet linens removed); POSITIONED sniffing; airway SUCTIONED only because obstructed (mouth before nose, bulb); STIMULATED (back rub, flick soles) — no response → begin PPV. PPV at 40-60/min with ROOM AIR (21%) for term within GOLDEN MINUTE; PULSE OX on RIGHT WRIST (preductal) + ECG. HR not rising initially → MR SOPA executed in order — M (mask seal) / R (reposition airway) / S (suction mouth/nose) / O (open mouth) / P (pressure ↑) / A (alternative airway = intubation); PPV became effective; HR remained <60 after 30 sec → CHEST COMPRESSIONS at 3:1 (TWO-THUMB encircling, lower 1/3 sternum, ~1/3 AP depth, 90 comp + 30 breaths = 120 events/min); FiO2 increased to 100% w/ compressions. HR still <60 after 60 sec of effective compressions + PPV → UMBILICAL VEIN CATHETER placed (fastest newborn access); EPINEPHRINE 0.02 mg/kg IV (0.2 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL) given; volume NS bolus considered. HR rose to 120 with ROSC + spontaneous respirations returning. THERMOREGULATION preserved (no hyperthermia). Apgar 3/7/8. Therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility screened (≥36 wk + <6 h of birth + evidence of HIE on standardized exam + cord gas). SBAR hand-off to NICU. Family communicated with honestly + brought to bedside as feasible. TEAM DEBRIEF held — what went well + what to do differently + chaplaincy/peer support offered. NEGATIVES (correct practice): did NOT start compressions before effective PPV (newborn bradycardia is almost always hypoxia → fix ventilation first), did NOT use 30:2 (newborn = 3:1), did NOT start 100% O2 in term (start 21%), did NOT skip right-wrist preductal pulse ox, did NOT forget MR SOPA, did NOT routinely intubate-suction non-vigorous meconium newborn, did NOT allow hyperthermia, did NOT over-explain at bedside, did NOT skip debrief.
providerOrders: PRE-DELIVERY: NRP team huddle for emergent cesarean with Category III FHR tracing; assign closed-loop roles (team leader, airway/RT, chest-compression provider, vascular-access/medications, documenter/recorder, family liaison); verify equipment (pre-warmed radiant warmer, warm blankets, plastic wrap for preterm < 32 weeks, suction with bulb/catheter, size-appropriate BMV, ETT 2.5/3.0/3.5, functioning laryngoscope, pulse oximeter, ECG, UVC tray, epinephrine, normal saline); confirm gestational age, weight, and maternal/fetal risk factors

AT BIRTH: rapid initial assessment (term? tone? breathing/crying?); bring to radiant warmer if any NO; initial steps about 30 seconds — WARM, DRY (vigorous; first stimulation; remove wet linens; plastic wrap without drying for preterm < 32 weeks), POSITION sniffing, CLEAR airway only if obstructed (mouth before nose), STIMULATE; evaluate breathing and heart rate (auscultate 6 seconds × 10 or use ECG)

POSITIVE-PRESSURE VENTILATION (PPV): begin within the Golden Minute at 40-60 breaths/min for HR < 100 or apnea/gasping; ROOM AIR (FiO2 21%) for term infants (FiO2 21-30% for preterm); place pulse oximeter on the RIGHT WRIST (preductal) and ECG for accurate HR; the best sign of effective PPV is a RISING HEART RATE
MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective: M (mask seal), R (reposition airway), S (suction mouth/nose), O (open mouth), P (pressure increase), A (alternative airway — intubation or laryngeal mask)

CHEST COMPRESSIONS: if HR remains < 60 after 30 seconds of EFFECTIVE PPV — two-thumb-encircling-hands technique on the lower third of the sternum at a 3:1 ratio (3 compressions : 1 breath) for 120 events per minute (90 compressions + 30 breaths), depth about 1/3 of the AP chest diameter; intubate or place an alternative airway; switch to FiO2 100% once compressions begin; reassess HR after 60 seconds of compressions + PPV
VASCULAR ACCESS: umbilical vein catheter (UVC) as the fastest access in the newborn; intraosseous (IO) if UVC fails

EPINEPHRINE: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (0.2 mL/kg of the 0.1 mg/mL solution) for HR < 60 after compressions + PPV; endotracheal 0.1 mg/kg may be used briefly while IV/IO is being established but is less reliable; repeat every 3-5 minutes as needed
VOLUME: normal saline 10 mL/kg IV/IO if hypovolemia is suspected (blood loss/abruption/cord accident); blood may be used if available
THERMOREGULATION: maintain radiant warmer + pre-warmed blankets; plastic wrap for preterm < 32 weeks; avoid hyperthermia
THERAPEUTIC HYPOTHERMIA SCREENING: screen for eligibility (≥ 36 weeks gestation, within 6 hours of birth, with evidence of hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy on standardized exam ± blood gas)
HAND-OFF + FAMILY + DEBRIEF: SBAR hand-off to NICU; communicate honestly and briefly with the family — bring to the bedside as feasible; bereavement/chaplaincy readiness; team debrief after the event

DO NOT: start chest compressions before PPV is effective; use a 30:2 ratio (newborn is 3:1); start at 100% O2 in a term newborn (start at 21% and escalate); skip the right-wrist preductal pulse oximeter; forget MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective; routinely intubate and tracheally suction a non-vigorous meconium-stained newborn (no longer routine); allow hyperthermia; over-explain clinical details to the family at the bedside; skip the team debrief

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.inst: Select ALL findings/considerations that are clinically significant.

opts.text: High-risk delivery (emergent cesarean for Category III FHR + maternal fever) → anticipate need for newborn resuscitation; pre-delivery huddle + equipment check + closed-loop role assignment before the baby arrives
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — preparation is the first leadership act. RN role: brief + delegate + verify.

opts.text: Rapid initial assessment (term? tone? breathing/crying?) — if any NO, bring to the radiant warmer for initial steps (~30 sec: warm + dry + position + clear airway only if obstructed + stimulate)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — the algorithm starts here. RN role: initial assessment + initial steps.

opts.text: For HR < 100 or apnea/gasping, begin PPV within the Golden Minute at 40-60/min with ROOM AIR (21%) for term infants; right-wrist preductal pulse ox + ECG; the best sign of effective PPV is a RISING HR
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — PPV is the most important intervention. RN role: effective PPV.

opts.text: If HR is not rising, perform MR SOPA in order — Mask seal, Reposition airway, Suction mouth/nose, Open mouth, Pressure ↑, Alternative airway
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — MR SOPA is the single most-tested corrective sequence. RN role: troubleshoot PPV.

opts.text: If HR remains < 60 after 30 sec of EFFECTIVE PPV, begin chest compressions (two-thumb encircling, lower 1/3 sternum, ~1/3 AP depth) at a 3:1 ratio (90 compressions + 30 breaths = 120 events/min) with FiO2 100%
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — compressions only after effective PPV; ratio is 3:1, not 30:2. RN role: correct compressions.

opts.text: If HR remains < 60 after 60 sec of compressions + PPV, place an umbilical vein catheter and give epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV (0.2 mL/kg of the 0.1 mg/mL solution); repeat q3-5 min; consider volume 10 mL/kg if hypovolemia suspected; preserve thermoregulation; screen for therapeutic hypothermia
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — medication pathway + thermoregulation + HIE screening. RN role: UVC + epinephrine + volume + thermoregulation + screen.

opts.text: Start chest compressions immediately at birth for any HR < 100, before PPV is effective
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Compressions start only if HR remains < 60 after 30 seconds of EFFECTIVE PPV; newborn bradycardia is almost always hypoxia from inadequate ventilation — fix ventilation first.

opts.text: Use a 30:2 compression-to-ventilation ratio with 100% O2 from the start in a term newborn
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. The newborn ratio is 3:1 (90 comp + 30 breaths = 120 events/min); start with FiO2 21% in a term infant and increase to 100% when compressions begin.

opts.text: Approximately 10% of newborns need help to begin breathing and about 1% need extensive resuscitation; pre-delivery huddle + equipment check + closed-loop role assignment are core leadership steps
opts.rat: CORRECT — epidemiology + preparation principle. Foundation for readiness.

opts.text: Initial steps (warm + dry + position + clear airway PRN + stimulate) are completed in about 30 seconds; the best sign of effective PPV is a rising HR (chest rise reassures but HR is the metric)
opts.rat: CORRECT — initial-steps and effectiveness principle. Foundation for the algorithm.

opts.text: MR SOPA (Mask, Reposition, Suction, Open mouth, Pressure, Alternative airway) is the corrective sequence when PPV is ineffective
opts.rat: CORRECT — MR SOPA principle. Foundation for troubleshooting.

opts.text: Compressions begin if HR < 60 after 30 seconds of EFFECTIVE PPV; preferred technique is two-thumb encircling on the lower third of the sternum at a 3:1 ratio with FiO2 100%
opts.rat: CORRECT — compression principle. Foundation for correct technique and ratio.

opts.text: Epinephrine is 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (0.2 mL/kg of the 0.1 mg/mL solution) via UVC (fastest newborn access); ET 0.1 mg/kg is a less-reliable bridge; repeat every 3-5 minutes; volume 10 mL/kg if hypovolemia is suspected
opts.rat: CORRECT — medication principle. Foundation for pharmacologic step.

opts.text: Thermoregulation is preserved throughout (avoid hyperthermia, which worsens HIE); therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility (≥ 36 weeks, < 6 h of birth, evidence of HIE) is screened after stabilization; SBAR hand-off to NICU; honest family communication; team debrief after
opts.rat: CORRECT — thermoregulation + HIE + hand-off + family/debrief. Foundation for full response.

opts.text: All meconium-stained non-vigorous newborns require routine endotracheal intubation and tracheal suctioning at birth
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Routine ET intubation/suctioning of non-vigorous meconium-stained newborns is no longer recommended; intervene only as needed for effective ventilation.

opts.text: Allowing the newborn to become hyperthermic during resuscitation improves outcomes
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Hyperthermia worsens HIE outcomes; preserve thermoregulation and avoid overheating.

opts.text: Pre-delivery huddle + equipment check + closed-loop role assignment before birth
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Preparation determines speed and quality at the actual event. RN role: brief + delegate + verify.

opts.text: Initial assessment + initial steps within ~30 seconds; begin PPV within the Golden Minute at 40-60/min with room air (term) for HR < 100 or apnea/gasping; right-wrist preductal pulse ox + ECG
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Airway + ventilation are the highest-impact bedside actions. RN role: PPV within the Golden Minute.

opts.text: MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective; intubate/LMA at the 'A' step if needed
opts.rat: #3 — Troubleshoot ventilation before escalating to compressions. RN role: MR SOPA.

opts.text: Compressions at 3:1 (two-thumb encircling) when HR < 60 after 30 sec of effective PPV; FiO2 100%; reassess HR after 60 sec
opts.rat: #4 — Compressions support circulation while ventilation is reversing hypoxia. RN role: correct compressions.

opts.text: Place UVC and give epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV; consider volume; preserve thermoregulation; screen for therapeutic hypothermia; SBAR hand-off to NICU; honest family communication; team debrief
opts.rat: #5 — Medication + post-resuscitation care + family + debrief complete the response. RN role: UVC + epi + thermoregulation + handoff + family + debrief.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH action, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this resuscitation.

opts.text: Hold a pre-delivery huddle; assign closed-loop roles; verify pre-warmed radiant warmer + suction + BMV + ETT sizes 2.5/3.0/3.5 + laryngoscope + pulse ox + ECG + UVC tray + epinephrine + normal saline
opts.rat: Preparation is the first leadership act. RN role: huddle + roles + equipment.

opts.text: Perform rapid initial assessment + initial steps (warm + dry + position + clear airway only if obstructed + stimulate); evaluate breathing and HR (auscultate 6 sec × 10 or ECG)
opts.rat: The algorithm's first 30 seconds. RN role: initial assessment + initial steps.

opts.text: Begin PPV within the Golden Minute at 40-60 breaths/min with ROOM AIR (21%) for a term newborn for HR < 100 or apnea/gasping; place pulse oximeter on the RIGHT WRIST and use ECG
opts.rat: PPV is the highest-impact intervention. RN role: effective PPV + preductal SpO2 + ECG.

opts.text: Perform MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective (Mask seal, Reposition airway, Suction mouth/nose, Open mouth, Pressure ↑, Alternative airway)
opts.rat: Corrects the most common cause of failed PPV. RN role: MR SOPA.

opts.text: Start chest compressions at a 3:1 ratio with two-thumb encircling on the lower third of the sternum and FiO2 100% if HR remains < 60 after 30 sec of effective PPV; reassess after 60 sec
opts.rat: Correct compressions only after effective PPV. RN role: 3:1 compressions.

opts.text: Place an umbilical vein catheter and give epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO if HR remains < 60 after compressions; consider volume 10 mL/kg if hypovolemia suspected; preserve thermoregulation; screen for therapeutic hypothermia; SBAR hand-off + family communication + debrief
opts.rat: Medications + post-resuscitation + family + debrief complete the response. RN role: UVC + epi + volume + thermoregulation + handoff.

opts.text: Start chest compressions immediately at birth for any HR < 100 before PPV is effective
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — compressions begin only if HR < 60 after 30 sec of EFFECTIVE PPV. Newborn bradycardia is almost always hypoxia — fix ventilation first.

opts.text: Use a 30:2 compression-to-ventilation ratio with 100% O2 from the start for a term newborn
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — newborn ratio is 3:1; start FiO2 21% in a term infant and escalate to 100% when compressions begin.

opts.text: Routinely intubate and tracheally suction a non-vigorous meconium-stained newborn at birth
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — no longer routine; intervene only as needed for effective ventilation.

opts.text: Allow the newborn to become hyperthermic during resuscitation to 'support metabolism'
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — hyperthermia worsens HIE outcomes; preserve thermoregulation and avoid overheating.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of the nurse's actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Pre-delivery huddle + closed-loop role assignment + equipment verification
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Preparation determines speed and quality at the event. RN role: brief + delegate + verify.

opts.text: Initial assessment + initial steps within ~30 sec; begin PPV within the Golden Minute at 40-60/min with room air (term) for HR < 100 or apnea/gasping; right-wrist preductal pulse ox + ECG
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Airway + ventilation are the highest-impact bedside actions. RN role: PPV within the Golden Minute.

opts.text: MR SOPA when PPV is ineffective (intubate/LMA at A if needed)
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Troubleshoot ventilation before escalating. RN role: MR SOPA.

opts.text: Chest compressions at 3:1 (two-thumb encircling) with FiO2 100% if HR < 60 after 30 sec of effective PPV; reassess after 60 sec
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Correct compressions only after effective PPV. RN role: compressions.

opts.text: Place UVC and give epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV; consider volume; preserve thermoregulation; screen for therapeutic hypothermia; SBAR hand-off + family + debrief
opts.rat: #5 — Closing. Medications + thermoregulation + handoff + family + debrief complete the response. RN role: meds + handoff + debrief.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each action/outcome, classify as APPROPRIATE or INAPPROPRIATE.

opts.text: The team pre-briefed before delivery, assigned closed-loop roles, and verified equipment; the radiant warmer was pre-heated and warm blankets ready
opts.rat: Preparation done correctly. Model leadership.

opts.text: Initial steps were completed in about 30 seconds and PPV at 40-60/min with room air was started within the Golden Minute when the newborn remained apneic with HR ~70; pulse oximeter was placed on the right wrist and ECG attached
opts.rat: Correct PPV with the right monitor placement. Correct trajectory.

opts.text: When PPV was not effective, MR SOPA was performed in order; an alternative airway was placed when needed and PPV became effective; FiO2 escalated only when compressions began
opts.rat: Correct troubleshooting and oxygen escalation. Correct trajectory.

opts.text: Compressions at 3:1 with two-thumb encircling were started only after 30 sec of effective PPV with HR still < 60; UVC placed and epinephrine 0.02 mg/kg IV given when HR remained < 60 after 60 sec of compressions + PPV; ROSC achieved; thermoregulation preserved; SBAR hand-off to NICU; family communication + team debrief
opts.rat: Full sequence executed correctly. Ideal trajectory.

opts.text: Alternative scenario — chest compressions were started immediately at birth for HR ~70 before PPV was even effective; the newborn's hypoxia was not addressed and HR continued to fall
opts.rat: ⚠️ Newborn bradycardia is almost always hypoxia — fix ventilation first. Compressions start only if HR < 60 after 30 sec of effective PPV.

opts.text: Alternative scenario — a 30:2 ratio was used with 100% O2 from the start in a term newborn; the rhythm was uncoordinated and oxygen use was inappropriate for a term infant
opts.rat: ⚠️ Newborn ratio is 3:1; start FiO2 21% in a term infant and escalate when compressions begin.

opts.text: Alternative scenario — the non-vigorous meconium-stained newborn was routinely intubated and tracheally suctioned before any PPV; effective ventilation was delayed
opts.rat: ⚠️ Routine intubation/suctioning of non-vigorous meconium-stained newborns is no longer recommended; intervene only as needed for effective ventilation.

opts.text: Alternative scenario — the team allowed hyperthermia during resuscitation; HIE outcome was worsened
opts.rat: ⚠️ Hyperthermia worsens HIE outcomes; preserve thermoregulation and avoid overheating.

opts.text: Alternative scenario — the family was given a lengthy technical explanation at the bedside during compressions and the hand-off to NICU skipped SBAR; communication failed
opts.rat: ⚠️ Honest brief updates by the leader or family liaison; SBAR hand-off prevents transition errors.

opts.text: After the event, the team held a structured debrief identifying what went well and what to improve; chaplaincy and peer support were offered; therapeutic-hypothermia eligibility was screened and the family was updated honestly
opts.rat: Closes the response for the team and the family. Ideal outcome.
